document: 1. FSCA Conduct Standard 1 of 2025 - Requirements for FIs providing FE initiatives

sections.heading: Definitions
sections.summary: This section defines key terms used in the Conduct Standard, including 'Authority', 'evaluation', 'financial customer', 'financial education', 'financial education initiative', 'monitoring', 'service provider', 'specific financial product or service', and 'target group'. These definitions clarify the scope and application of the standard.
sections.dtia_summary: Understanding these definitions is crucial for a DTIA to correctly identify the scope of 'financial education initiatives', the 'financial customers' involved, and the roles of 'service providers' and the 'Authority', ensuring accurate assessment of data flows and responsibilities.
sections.source_quote: In this Schedule, "the Act" means the Financial Sector Regulation Act, 2017 () and any word or expression to which a meaning has been assigned in the Act bears the meaning so assigned to it, and unless the context otherwise indicates – "Authority" means the Financial Sector Conduct Authority as defined in section 1 of the Act;

sections.heading: Application, purpose and proportional application
sections.summary: This Conduct Standard applies when a financial institution, or its service provider, offers financial education initiatives. Its purpose is to protect financial customers by ensuring appropriate initiatives and to mandate reasonable steps for governance and oversight by financial institutions.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes the applicability and objectives of the Conduct Standard, guiding a DTIA on when and why the requirements for financial education initiatives, including those involving third parties, must be assessed for compliance and customer protection.

requirements.text: This Conduct Standard is only applicable where a financial institution provides or offers a financial education initiative, including the provision of a financial education initiative by a service provider.
requirements.trigger: when a financial institution provides or offers a financial education initiative

requirements.text: A financial institution providing financial education initiatives takes reasonable steps to ensure appropriate standards of behaviour, governance and oversight when developing content, implementing, monitoring and evaluating a financial education initiative.

A financial institution providing financial education initiatives takes reasonable steps to ensure appropriate standards of behaviour, governance and oversight when reporting on the effectiveness, efficiency and appropriateness of a financial education initiative, including reporting to the Authority.

The requirements in this Conduct Standard, where appropriate, may be applied in a manner that is proportional to the nature, size, complexity and risk profile of the financial institution concerned as well as the nature and target group of the relevant financial education initiative.
requirements.trigger: when applying the requirements of this Conduct Standard

sections.source_quote: This Conduct Standard is only applicable where a financial institution provides or offers a financial education initiative, including the provision of a financial education initiative by a service provider. The purpose of this Conduct Standard is aimed at ensuring the protection of financial customers by ensuring that financial education initiatives provided by financial institutions are appropriate.

sections.heading: Governance arrangements
sections.summary: Financial institutions must establish governance arrangements for financial education initiatives, including ongoing oversight, reporting to the Authority, ensuring employee and service provider competence, and extending governance to service providers. Financial institutions retain full responsibility for regulatory obligations.
sections.dtia_summary: This section is critical for a DTIA as it mandates robust governance, oversight, and accountability for financial education initiatives, especially when third-party service providers are involved, directly impacting data handling and compliance responsibilities.

requirements.text: A financial institution must have appropriate governance arrangements in place to oversee the design and implementation of financial education initiatives.

The governance arrangements must include establishing and implementing appropriate oversight arrangements to monitor and review the design, suitability and effectiveness of its financial education initiatives on an ongoing basis.

A financial institution must have appropriate systems, processes, policies and controls in place to ensure appropriate reporting on financial education initiatives, pre- and post-implementation, to the Authority.

A financial institution must take reasonable steps to ensure that the relevant employee and service provider responsible for the design and implementation of a financial education initiative possesses the necessary skills, knowledge and expertise to fulfil their functions.

A financial institution must take reasonable steps to ensure that the governance arrangements referred to in this paragraph also apply to a service provider, including that it obtains sufficient information from that service provider, to ensure that these financial education initiatives are, and remain, effective in achieving the intended outcomes.

A financial institution retains responsibility and accountability for all regulatory obligations pertaining to the provision of financial education initiatives as set out in this Conduct Standard, regardless of whether such financial education initiative is performed by the financial institution itself or by a service provider.

sections.source_quote: A financial institution must have appropriate governance arrangements in place to oversee the design and implementation of financial education initiatives. A financial institution retains responsibility and accountability for all regulatory obligations pertaining to the provision of financial education initiatives as set out in this Conduct Standard, regardless of whether such financial education initiative is performed by the financial institution itself or by a service provider.

sections.heading: Design and development requirements for financial education initiatives
sections.summary: Financial education initiatives should enable informed financial decisions and encourage positive customer behaviour. Content must be objective, impartial, targeted, distinct from marketing, and brand agnostic, though it may explain financial concepts.
sections.dtia_summary: This section guides the design and content development of financial education initiatives, which is relevant for a DTIA to ensure that customer information is used ethically and that the initiatives themselves are not disguised marketing.

requirements.text: Financial education initiative provided by a financial institution should aim to enable natural persons and small, medium and micro-enterprises to make more informed decisions, about their own finances and financial well-being or the finances and financial well-being of others that fall under their responsibility.
requirements.trigger: when providing a financial education initiative

requirements.text: Financial education initiative provided by a financial institution should aim to encourage positive customer behaviour, influence behavioural change and contribute to long-term skills development in respect of financial decision making.
requirements.trigger: when providing a financial education initiative

requirements.text: A financial institution may use any delivery platform for a financial education initiative, provided that the delivery platform is appropriate for the type of financial education initiative and the identified target group, to ensure appropriate and effective reach.
requirements.trigger: when using a delivery platform for a financial education initiative

requirements.text: The content of a financial education initiative provided by a financial institution must be based on objective and impartial information, generally accepted methodologies, research and trends.
requirements.trigger: when providing a financial education initiative

requirements.text: The content of a financial education initiative provided by a financial institution must be targeted suitably at the appropriate target group, taking into account the identified needs of the target group and financial inclusion objectives.
requirements.trigger: when providing a financial education initiative

requirements.text: The content of a financial education initiative provided by a financial institution must be distinct from any brand specific financial product or financial service sales process or marketing campaign.
requirements.trigger: when providing a financial education initiative

requirements.text: The content of a financial education initiative provided by a financial institution must be brand agnostic, but may unpack the meaning of different types of financial concepts and terms related to a particular financial product or financial service, or categories of financial products or financial services.
requirements.trigger: when providing a financial education initiative

sections.source_quote: Financial education initiative provided by a financial institution should aim to enable natural persons and small, medium and micro-enterprises to make more informed decisions, about their own finances and financial well-being or the finances and financial well-being of others that fall under their responsibility. The content of a financial education initiative provided by a financial institution must be based on objective and impartial information, generally accepted methodologies, research and trends.

sections.heading: Location and logistics
sections.summary: This Conduct Standard is officially named 'Requirements for financial institutions providing financial education initiatives, 2025' and will become effective 12 months after its publication date.
sections.dtia_summary: This section provides the official title and effective date of the Conduct Standard, which is essential for a DTIA to ensure it is assessing compliance against the correct and currently applicable version of the legislation.
sections.source_quote: This Conduct Standard is called Requirements for financial institutions providing financial education initiatives, 2025 and comes into effect 12 months after the date of publication.
